summary: Production instance web-03 (i-0f1e2d3c4b5a69788, eu-west-2a) received 41 inbound SSH brute-force attempts over 3.5 hours from 203.0.113.42, a well-known malicious IP scoring 97/100 with 1,243 threat reports in 90 days and botnet/scanner tags. The connection to TCP/22 is currently unblocked, meaning the attack is ongoing. CloudTrail shows only a single benign S3 GetObject call from the instance's private IP — no evidence of successful compromise, privilege escalation, or lateral movement at this time. Isolating the instance's security group is the proportionate response; escalation to stop or snapshot the instance is not warranted unless post-compromise activity is subsequently detected.

evidence.observation: 41 inbound TCP/22 connection attempts from 203.0.113.42 to i-0f1e2d3c4b5a69788 (web-03, production) over ~3.5 hours (03:14–06:41 UTC); connection is NOT blocked; security group web-tier-ssh (sg-0aa11bb22cc33dd44) is permissive to this source.
203.0.113.42 reputation score 97/100 malicious; tagged ssh-bruteforce, botnet, scanner; 1,243 abuse reports in the past 90 days; active as recently as 2026-07-12 (today).

Only one CloudTrail event in 72 hrs for web-tier-profile: a single S3 GetObject from private IP 10.20.1.57 at 05:12 UTC. No credential misuse, no console logins, no lateral movement — no confirmed post-compromise activity.

proposed_actions.justification: IP 203.0.113.42 scores 97/100 malicious with 1,243 reports in 90 days (ssh-bruteforce, botnet tags). GuardDuty logged 41 unblocked brute-force attempts on TCP/22 over 3.5 hrs. No post-compromise CloudTrail activity found, so SG isolation is proportionate — stops the attack without halting the instance.
proposed_actions.risk_if_wrong: Legitimate SSH access to web-03 from authorised IPs will be blocked until rules are refined. Recoverable: re-add a scoped ingress rule once authorised source ranges are confirmed.
